premise: My half-sister, who visited our father once in eight years, pretended to reconcile when his dementia worsened and maneuvered him into signing over his $420,000 apartment building while he was sedated — she sold it for cash and dumped him in a state-funded nursing home. I found the medical records proving he couldn’t consent, won an elder-abuse suit with $500,000 in damages, and when she couldn’t pay, I accepted the deed to her own home in lieu of interest — then evicted her and converted it into a grief counseling center for fellow forgotten caregivers.

thumb.label: Thumbnail + metadata
thumb.detail: My Half-Sister Stole Dad's Dementia-Signed Building, Dumped Him. So I Turned Her
